name: pcs-facet-enrichment-pipeline
description: End-to-end faceted-search enrichment: take an Anglera Faceted Search Backfill export, run a coverage gap analysis, back-fill blank attributes with HIGH-confidence values scanned from the products' real source PDPs, then output a NetSuite-ready facet import. Built for a non-technical operator — Claude automates ingest, gap analysis, scraper config, QA, and NetSuite formatting; the operator only drops the file and double-clicks a one-click runner. v1.1.0: only the final NetSuite import CSV is uploaded to the shared Google Drive NS-import folder; every other output (gap report, backfill plan, PDP-filled xlsx/log, QA summary, dropdown-additions list) stays in the operator's local working folder. v1.2.0: single-value enforcement — only safety_rating, drive_size, and battery_compatibility may hold multiple comma-joined values; the NetSuite-format step now collapses every other facet (incl. Anglera pass-through fields like For Use On / For Use With / Material) to its first value, protecting numeric thousands separators (10,000 cps stays whole), and the scraper's multi gate is an explicit allowlist so a MULTI_SELECT mislabel in attributes (NN).csv can't leak multi-values. v1.3.0: adds an in-chat manual-review gate — the scraper now HOLDS uncertain values (retailer-sourced, normalization-adjusted, inferred-from-prose, out-of-dropdown, or multiple-candidate) in a review queue instead of writing them; the operator accepts/rejects each in an interactive chat artifact (default Accept, Accept-all/Reject-all), and only AUTO + accepted values are built into the NetSuite import (accepted out-of-dropdown values are also logged operator-approved). This replaces the old pre-NetSuite Yes/No gate.
